matches.rationale: Same Bash tool and same pre-PR formatting action: the extracted compound rule explicitly requires `make format` in the modified library's directory before creating a pull request. It also covers linting and testing, but under the 1:1 rule it is assigned to this single closest gold rule.
Same Edit tool and same target: the pattern blocks Sphinx-style double-backtick inline code formatting in source files, with evidence specifically limiting the instruction to docstrings and comments.

misses.note: The lint requirement is present only inside the compound extracted rule `run-format-lint-test-before-pr`, which is already assigned to g1 and cannot be reused under the 1:1 constraint.
The test-suite requirement is present only inside the compound extracted rule `run-format-lint-test-before-pr`, which is already assigned to g1 and cannot be reused under the 1:1 constraint.
Compiler self-reported this as uncompilable: "TEST=path/to/test.py make test usage note for running a single test file — describes an optional variable, not an obligation or prohibition".

The single-backtick instruction appears only in the evidence of `no-sphinx-double-backticks`, whose trigger and blocking pattern most closely match g5; that extracted rule cannot also be assigned to g6 under the 1:1 constraint.
